commit_scope: Prove direct application graph table mutation SQL as a Form-native carrier before mutable front-door route flips.

commands: cd api && python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_application_graph_node_port_form.py tests/test_ideas_router_form.py tests/test_spec_registry_router_form.py tests/test_native_auth_parity_form.py tests/test_graph_node_mutation_carrier_form.py

cd api && python3 -m ruff check tests/test_application_graph_node_port_form.py tests/test_ideas_router_form.py tests/test_spec_registry_router_form.py tests/test_native_auth_parity_form.py tests/test_graph_node_mutation_carrier_form.py
local_validation.summary: form/form-stdlib/application-graph-node-port.fk now carries application-table mutation SQL for graph_nodes, graph_node_revisions, and graph_edges. Create SQL inserts the node and a __create__ revision snapshot. Update SQL merges properties, sets updated_at, computes the next per-node revision number, records fields_changed, and snapshots the updated node. Delete SQL clears connected graph_edges before deleting the graph_nodes row. The Form band returned 1111.

known_limitations.observed: No POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/ideas or /api/spec-registry route is flipped in this slice.
The carrier emits SQL and exposes pg_exec binding functions. A later route-specific proof must execute against the application schema before a front-door flip.
Idea/spec response projection, cache invalidation, resonance re-attunement, and contributor-key last_used_at updates remain method-specific route-binding work.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push of this application graph SQL carrier commit and GitHub checks on PR #2586.

deploy_validation.notes: No public deploy attempted for this Form stdlib carrier slice.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Form can now express direct application graph table mutation SQL for create/update/delete semantics, including revision snapshots and edge cleanup. Existing public mutation endpoints keep current FastAPI behavior until method-specific route rows bind request bodies, response projection, cache invalidation, and live DB execution proof.
